mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a drop-in replacement for Redis and Memcached, offering significantly higher throughput and lower resource consumption. It is ideal for applications requiring high performance caching and data storage. Key trade-offs include the need for compatibility with existing Redis/Memcached clients and potential resource savings.

steps.title: Add the Dragonfly Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://charts.dragonflydb.io

```

> This is the official repository for Dragonfly charts.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Dragonfly to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

```

> This CRD defines the Dragonfly custom resource.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Dragonfly in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.37.0

```

> Ensure the namespace is created for Dragonfly.

steps.title: Create a Dragonfly instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Dragonfly custom resource to deploy an instance of Dragonfly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1alpha1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a Dragonfly instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1alpha1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dragonfly instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Dragonfly instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly

```

> You should see your Dragonfly instance listed with its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step will remove Dragonfly and its associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources may remain.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> This command removes the Dragonfly release.
> This command deletes the namespace and any remaining resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Dragonfly to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.37.0

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly

```

> Backup the current Dragonfly configuration.
> This command upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Verify that the Dragonfly instance is running the new version.
commands: kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dragonfly instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly instance may not have been created or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Check the Dragonfly custom resource with 'kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Dragonfly'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Dragonfly pods with 'kubectl logs -l app=dragonfly -n dragonfly'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the Dragonfly pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements and scale up if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions for Dragonfly may not have been installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the command: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new caching algorithms and improved performance metrics.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dragonfly instance listed with 'kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly' showing 'Running' status.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
